channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump administration pushes to get AI data centers on the grid faster
item.description: The Trump administration issued a proposal late Thursday that aims to help AI data centers get connected to the electric grid more quickly.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Hackers steal medical records and financial data from 1.2M patients in massive healthcare breach
item.description: A cyberattack on SimonMed Imaging exposed personal information of 1.2 million patients, including medical records, financial details and identity papers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anthropic signs billion-dollar deal with Google Cloud
item.description: US-based AI company Anthropic has signed a major deal with Google Cloud that is said to be worth tens of billions of dollars. As part of the deal, Anthropic will have access to up to one million of Google’s purpose-built Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) AI accelerators.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Shifting your organizational focus from MTTD to MTTC in 8 steps
item.description: I spend a lot of time with operations leaders who have excellent visibility … but disappointing outcomes. The paradigm is that we invest heavily in detection, correlation and dashboards, then depend on human heroics. It’s the smoke-alarm-without-a-sprinkler problem.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to watch Mexico City Grand Prix 2025 F1: free streams, preview, schedule, Verstappen quickest in practice as Norris and Piastri toil
item.description: All the ways to watch Mexico City Grand Prix live streams online and from anywhere, as Max Verstappen nears Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris in the title race.
item.dc:creator: Andy Murray, TechRadar

item.title: How Patmos set up an AI data center in 90 days
item.description: Data center hosting and compute services provider Patmos set up and deployed a new AI data center in just 90 days, when it usually takes two to three years to set up a colocation facility. All it needed was an unused printing press in the heart of Kansas City.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel posts profit even as it struggles to regain market share
item.description: Intel posted profits in the latest quarter as the one-time America technology icon struggles to regain market share.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AWS reveals new cloud incident reporting tool, with almost zero sense of irony
item.description: AWS CloudWatch will get new incident report generation tool - days after an AWS outage affected parts of the internet.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Living in giant moon glass spheres could be our future
item.description: California company Skyeports creates self-healing glass spheres from Moon regolith that generate solar power and support plant growth for sustainable lunar living.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delete the fake VPN app stealing Android users' money
item.description: Cleafy researchers discover fake VPN streaming app Mobdro Pro that installs Klopatra banking Trojan, giving attackers full control over Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google’s Quantum chip claims 13,000x speed advantage over supercomputers
item.description: Google Quantum AI has demonstrated what it describes as a verifiable quantum advantage — running a new algorithm on quantum hardware that outperforms classical supercomputers by a factor of 13,000.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Post outage, AWS adds automated incident reporting to its CloudWatch service
item.description: In response to a major outage on Monday, AWS has added an automated incident-generating capability within its CloudWatch service for reporting use cases.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A practitioner’s primer on deterministic application modernization
item.description: Large organizations rarely have just a handful of applications. They have thousands, often representing billions of lines of code. These code bases span decades of frameworks, libraries, and shifting best practices.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Inside Google’s multi-architecture revolution: Axion Arm joins x86 in production clusters
item.description: While x86 has been dominant for decades, a new migration project at Google represents a significant shift to more mixed architectures.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Serious vulnerability found in Rust library
item.description: Developers creating projects in the Rust programming language, as well as IT leaders with Rust-based applications in their environments, should pay attention to a serious vulnerability found in one of the programming language’s libraries.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This is the world’s first vertical take-off AI-piloted fighter jet
item.description: San Diego-based Shield AI is developing a first of its kind fighter jet: a 2,000-mile-range pilotless plane that takes off and lands vertically and uses artificial intelligence to fly itself, even when adversaries jam navigation and communication systems.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Reddit sues AI company Perplexity and others for 'industrial-scale' scraping of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Modernization with purpose: How federal health agencies can align tech with mission
item.description: Aligning tech, data and workforce to improve health outcomes starts with asking why, Maximus’ Monica Rosser says. The post Modernization with purpose: How federal health agencies can align tech with mission first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kandji becomes Iru, opens MDM for Windows and Android
item.description: Apple device management and security company Kandji has changed its name to Iru, reflecting a new approach to what it does while opening its offer up to Windows and Android.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
